title: Differentiation II: Chain, Product and Quotient Rules
description: Differentiating composite, product and quotient functions; the standard derivatives of sin x, cos x, tan x, e^x and ln x; connected rates of change; and using stationary points and the second derivative to sketch curves.
objectives: Differentiate a composite function f(g(x)) using the chain rule
Differentiate a product uv using the product rule and a quotient u/v using the quotient rule
Recall and use the standard derivatives of sin x, cos x, tan x, e^x and ln x
Apply the chain rule to connected rates of change problems
Find stationary points by solving f'(x) = 0 and classify them using the second derivative
Combine these techniques to sketch the graph of a function

lessons.title: Standard derivatives and connected rates of change
